free_text: Analog (p,n) cross section of the zirconium isotopes
at 18 and 25 MeV
Cyclograaff accelerator
Energy spread represents an average of energy losses
in target.
Neutron time-of-flight spectrometry.
16 NE213 scintillator behind water collimators.
Approved by R.W. Bauer, 26 August 2002.
Converted from data set S0027; updated
and data added.
Zirconium targets were self-supporting metallic
foils of 2.38-cm diameter and nominal thicknesses
ranging from 5.3 to 6.2 mg/cm**2. Isotopic enrichment
Isobaric analog state.
Errors include statistical contributions
and estimated peak fitting error.  Minimum error
taken as 7% to account for uncertainty in absolute
detector efficiency.
Data tables received from E. Bauer, 12 July 2002.
Zirconium targets were self-supporting metallic
foils of 2.38-cm diameter and nominal thicknesses
ranging from 5.3 to 6.2 mg/cm**2. Isotopic enrichment
Isobaric analog state.
Errors determined from both the relative
uncertainty in data points and from goodness of
polynomial series fit to data
Data taken from Table I in reference.
Zirconium targets were self-supporting metallic
foils of 2.38-cm diameter and nominal thicknesses
ranging from 5.3 to 6.2 mg/cm**2. Enriched to 88.5%
91Zr.
Isobaric analog state.
Errors include statistical contributions
and estimated peak fitting error.  Minimum error
taken as 7% to account for uncertainty in absolute
detector efficiency.
Data tables received from E. Bauer, 12 July 2002.
Zirconium targets were self-supporting metallic
foils of 2.38-cm diameter and nominal thicknesses
ranging from 5.3 to 6.2 mg/cm**2. Enriched to 88.5%
91Zr.
Isobaric analog state.
Not corrected for principal contaminent 90Zr, since
90Zr and 91Zr cross sections are essentially equal.
Errors determined from both the relative
uncertainty in data points and from goodness of
polynomial series fit to data
